extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIC's Jumbo-Visma won the 23km TTT by 13 seconds over Ineos, with GESINK crossing first to claim the leader's jersey on home roads in Netherlands.
tactical_patterns: Groupama-FDJ sacrificed riders early to maximize Yates' time advantage, losing teammates but posting surprisingly fast time for climber squad.
QuickStep's inexperience cost them victory despite strong ride; young squad couldn't manage pacing to keep all riders together efficiently.
Jumbo-Visma brought flat specialists Eekhoff, Teunissen, Dennis alongside Roglic showing TTT-specific roster construction rather than pure climbing support.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: BikeExchange/Groupama squad posted unexpectedly competitive TTT times despite being climber-focused rosters, suggesting strong form heading into mountains
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: QuickStep went too hard early with Evenepoel pulling strongly, revealing possible overconfidence in short TTT pacing discipline
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: 13-second margin over Ineos shows Jumbo brought TTT-optimized roster depth, not just GC protection squad
rider_signals.GESINK Robert: positive: Team chose him to cross first for sentimental leader's jersey at age 36, revealing team morale prioritization over pure performance optics
